claims.text: Some scientists believe time is non-linear and that past, present and future coexist in parallel dimensions, which is the physical premise for time slips.

evidence.quote:
some
scientists believe that time is not
linear and that the past present and
future coexist simultaneously in
parallel
Dimensions

claims.rationale: Episode's physical-framing premise. Block-universe and many-worlds are live physics interpretations but the specific 'parallel dimensions coexist' phrasing is interpretive rather than a measured fact. directTruth omitted — no calibrated basis.

claims.text: In 1901 Oxford academics Charlotte Moberly and Eleanor Jourdain time-slipped at Versailles to 1792 and encountered Marie Antoinette.

evidence.quote:
one of the most famous Time
Slips happened in 1901 charlot mly and
Elanor jordane two Oxford academics were
touring the Palace of RSA in France

their research LED them
to believe they've been transported to
1792 just before the fall of the French
monarchy

claims.rationale: The women did have some real experience at Versailles and published a book. Literal time-slip reading is dismantled in the verdict: notes didn't match for three months, dates drifted, and Marie Antoinette was verifiably elsewhere by the claimed year.

claims.text: Carl Jung had a time slip in 1930s Ravenna where he vividly observed Baptistery of Neon mosaics that had been destroyed by fire centuries earlier.

evidence.quote:
eventually young learned these mosaics
had existed but were destroyed in a fire
centuries ago yet he described them
perfectly

claims.rationale: Jung's Ravenna anecdote is in his autobiography but is explicitly debunked in the verdict as secondhand and wrong-church. directTruth omitted — the literal paranormal claim is unfalsifiable.

claims.text: In 1979 two British couples stayed at an oil-lamp-lit French hotel that had vanished on their return trip, with no photographs of it surviving on the developed film.

evidence.quote:
on their return trip they decided to
stay at the hotel again but couldn't
find it locals had never heard of
it

claims.rationale: Host's verdict points out internal impossibilities (1979 cash, 1979 Chrysler) and shifting details across retellings. No primary documentation.

claims.text: In 1999 a tourist named Sebastian Reigns ate at a 1960s-style NYC diner that turned out to have burned down in 1974 and stood boarded-up for 25 years.

evidence.quote:
the diner had
a kitchen fire in 1974 destroying the
place and the building had been empty
for over 25
years

claims.rationale: Host explicitly rejects this story in the verdict — no such person exists and every source is a paranormal aggregator.

claims.text: In 1953 plumber's apprentice Harry Martindale watched Roman soldiers march through the basement of the Treasurer's House in York, visible only from the knees up.

evidence.quote:
a group of Roman
soldiers emerged from the wall marching
in formation they wore green tunics and
carried round Shields

claims.rationale: Famous York paranormal report. Host's verdict admits wanting to believe it but notes the story was unshared until years later. directTruth omitted — literal apparition claim is unfalsifiable.

claims.text: A later excavation revealed a Roman road about 18 inches below the Treasurer's House basement floor, exactly matching Martindale's knees-up visibility and identifying details he could not have known.

evidence.quote:
in the 1990s Harry's story took
an unexpected turn an excavation
revealed an ancient Roman Road running
right beneath that basement about 18 in
below the floor

claims.rationale: York's Roman-road network and Eboracum legionary presence are well-documented archaeology. The specific sub-basement depth is plausibly true; the knees-up correspondence is a striking but partly anecdotal detail.

dependencies.rationale: The physical excavation corroborates details Martindale described — the remaining evidentiary weight of the sighting hangs on this.

claims.text: Liverpool, specifically Bold Street, is the world's most-reported hot spot for time slips.

evidence.quote:
Bold Street has
become a hot spot where people are
momentarily transported to another time

claims.rationale: As a folklore-distribution claim, true: Bold Street has the densest collection of English-language time-slip reports, confirmed by paranormal communities and aggregator sites. Whether any report reflects reality is a separate question.

claims.text: In 1996 Frank, a retired police officer, experienced Bold Street transform into the 1950s and walked into a women's shop called Crips that had actually occupied that address in the 1950s.

evidence.quote:
Crips was indeed a
women's clothing shop that existed on
Bold Street in the ' 50s right where
Dylan's was now

claims.rationale: Only Julie French claims to be the co-witness; everything else is self-reported decades later. Host says veracity 'comes down to whether or not you believe her'.

claims.text: In May 2011 a 17-year-old named Imagin shopped at a Mothercare store on Bold Street that turned out to have been an HSBC bank for years.

evidence.quote:
the mother
care store closed years ago and that the
building was now an HSBC Bank

claims.rationale: Host's verdict: Mothercare never had a Bold Street location. Only source is a single website — no primary record.

claims.text: Telluric currents interacting with Liverpool's concentric underground rails centered on Bold Street create time vortices.

evidence.quote:
concentric circles of high
voltage rails centered on Bold Street
these rails might be creating time
vortices

claims.rationale: Telluric currents are real geophysics but the premise (concentric Liverpool rails) is factually wrong per the verdict; the leap from currents to time-bending vortices has no physics support.

claims.text: Einstein's relativity shows time is not constant — it dilates with speed and gravity — and wormholes could theoretically connect different points in time.

evidence.quote:
Einstein's theory of relativity
changed our understanding of time

theoretically wormholes could
allow instant travel between distant
points in space and maybe even different
points in time

claims.rationale: Time dilation is experimentally verified mainstream physics (GPS satellites require it). Wormhole solutions exist mathematically; traversable wormholes would require exotic negative-energy matter. Physics premise is solid; time-slip extrapolation is speculative.

claims.text: Marie Antoinette was demonstrably not at Versailles in 1792 — she left in October 1789 and was confined to Tuileries until August 1792, then imprisoned.

evidence.quote:
Maria Anette was at Versailles until
October 1789 but that's it

claims.rationale: Well-documented: the October 1789 Women's March forced the royal family to Paris (Tuileries), where they remained under restriction until August 1792. This chronology makes a literal 1792 Versailles sighting of Antoinette impossible.

dependencies.rationale: [debunks] Antoinette's documented 1789-1792 whereabouts make the alleged Versailles sighting impossible by chronology.

claims.text: The Moberly-Jourdain case is not a real time slip — the women waited three months to compare notes, their accounts contradicted each other, and the Society for Psychical Research dismissed it.

evidence.quote:
Charlotte and Elanor sent their
story to England's Society for psychical
research who said there is nothing to it

claims.rationale: Host explicit verdict. Convergent skeptical consensus (SPR dismissal; Sewell and later analyses) identifies memory-reconstruction over many edition-rewrites as the mechanism.

dependencies.rationale: [debunks] Directly rejects the literal time-slip reading on multiple concrete grounds.
The Antoinette chronology is the load-bearing concrete debunking point for this verdict.

claims.text: Jung's Ravenna mosaic story is debunked — secondhand, confused dates and locations, and Jung was in a different church that actually did have mosaics.

evidence.quote:
Carl Young didn't time travel his story
is All secondhand it confuses dates and
locations young was in a different
church that had mosaics so this one is
debunked

claims.rationale: Host presents this to reject the time-slip reading. Jung biographers (Shamdasani) have noted other Ravenna sites with mosaics matching Jung's description.

dependencies.rationale: [debunks] Rejects the paranormal reading on secondhand-provenance and wrong-church grounds.

claims.text: The 1979 Gisby/Simpson vanishing-hotel story fails internal consistency because 1970s cash would have been useless in an early-1900s hotel.

evidence.quote:
their cash would have been
useless

claims.rationale: Sound currency-anachronism argument: French francs were redenominated in 1960; earlier-era notes would have been recognizably obsolete. No primary documentation of the episode exists.

dependencies.rationale: [logical] Internal inconsistency — payment would have been impossible if they had traveled back.

claims.text: Sebastian Reigns never existed — every source for his NYC diner story is a paranormal website recycling the same tale.

evidence.quote:
no such person ever existed
the sources for this story are other
paranormal websites

claims.rationale: No primary source, no documented witness, no original venue record. Host's dismissal follows standard folklore-verification methodology.

dependencies.rationale: [debunks] Direct refutation — subject of the story is fictional.

claims.text: None of the specific Bold Street time-slip stories can be verified — all sources are other websites recycling the same tales.

evidence.quote:
Liverpool is definitely Ground Zero for
time slip stories but there's not a
shred of evidence for any of them

claims.rationale: Reflects actual sourcing picture: Liverpool time-slip lore circulates in paranormal forums without primary documentation, named witnesses, or business-register corroboration.

dependencies.rationale: [debunks] The Frank case is part of the unverifiable Bold Street canon.
[debunks] The Imagin case has no primary source — only a single website mention.

claims.text: The Liverpool underground rail system is not actually arranged in concentric circles — the schematic map just renders it that way for legibility.

evidence.quote:
the liver pool
underground is not actually arranged in
concentric circles they just made the
map look like that so it's easier to
read

claims.rationale: Verifiable against actual Merseyrail geography. Schematic transit maps universally compress geometry. Factual correction stands.

dependencies.rationale: [debunks] The concentric-rails premise of the telluric-vortex theory is factually wrong.

claims.text: Most time-slip experiences are made up, but we cannot rule them all out because we don't understand enough about the universe to declare them impossible.

evidence.quote:
it's safe to say that most time
slip experiences are made up but I can't
say all of them are

claims.rationale: Calibrated closing agnosticism. The 'most are made up' half is strongly supported by the preceding debunks; the 'can't rule all out' half is appropriate humility given open questions in physics interpretation.
